root_text.an4.123:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.123:1.4: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.123:1.5: Tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno brahmakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.123:1.7: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an4.123:1.8: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an4.123:1.9: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā sati.
root_text.an4.123:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ avitakkaṁ avicāraṁ samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.123:2.2: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.123:2.3: Tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno ābhassarānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.123:2.4: Ābhassarānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ dve kappā āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.123:2.5: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an4.123:2.6: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an4.123:2.7: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā sati.
root_text.an4.123:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo pītiyā ca virāgā upekkhako ca viharati sato ca sampajāno sukhañca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti yaṁ taṁ ariyā ācikkhanti: ‘upekkhako satimā sukhavihārī’ti tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.123:3.2: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.123:3.3: Tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno, kālaṁ kurumāno subhakiṇhānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.123:3.4: Subhakiṇhānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ cattāro kappā āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.123:3.5: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an4.123:3.6: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an4.123:3.7: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā sati.
root_text.an4.123:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.123:4.2: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an4.123:4.3: Tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno vehapphalānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.123:4.4: Vehapphalānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ pañca kappasatāni āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.123:4.5: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an4.123:4.6: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an4.123:4.7: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā sati.
root_text.an4.123:4.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.123:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.123:1.3: Take an individual who, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an4.123:1.4: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.123:1.5: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the Divinity’s host.
translation_text.an4.123:1.6: The lifespan of the gods of the Divinity’s host is one eon.
translation_text.an4.123:1.7: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an4.123:1.8: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an4.123:1.9: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person, that is, when there is a place of rebirth.
translation_text.an4.123:2.1: Furthermore, take an individual who, as the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, enters and remains in the second absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of immersion, with internal clarity and mind at one, without placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an4.123:2.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.123:2.3: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of streaming radiance.
translation_text.an4.123:2.4: The lifespan of the gods of streaming radiance is two eons.
translation_text.an4.123:2.5: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an4.123:2.6: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an4.123:2.7: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person, that is, when there is a place of rebirth.
translation_text.an4.123:3.1: Furthermore, take an individual who, with the fading away of rapture, enters and remains in the third absorption, where they meditate with equanimity, mindful and aware, personally experiencing the bliss of which the noble ones declare, ‘Equanimous and mindful, one meditates in bliss.’
translation_text.an4.123:3.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.123:3.3: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of universal beauty.
translation_text.an4.123:3.4: The lifespan of the gods of universal beauty is four eons.
translation_text.an4.123:3.5: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an4.123:3.6: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an4.123:3.7: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person, that is, when there is a place of rebirth.
translation_text.an4.123:4.1: Furthermore, take an individual who, giving up pleasure and pain, and ending former happiness and sadness, enters and remains in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness.
translation_text.an4.123:4.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.123:4.3: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of abundant fruit.
translation_text.an4.123:4.4: The lifespan of the gods of abundant fruit is five hundred eons.
translation_text.an4.123:4.5: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an4.123:4.6: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an4.123:4.7: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person, that is, when there is a place of rebirth.
translation_text.an4.123:4.8: These are the four individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.123:1.9: yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā sati → yadidaṁ gatiyā uppattiyā sati (pts1ed); yadidaṁ cutiyā upapattiyā cāti (mr)
